2606.22061.title: NL2Scratch: An Executable Benchmark and Evaluation for Block-Based Programming
2606.22061.paper_summary: Block-based programming environments such as Scratch are widely used in early programming education, yet natural-language-to-code (NL2Code) research has focused primarily on text-based languages. Scratch programs are event-driven, visually compositional, and distributed across concurrent scripts, making conventional NL2Code assumptions and evaluation insufficient. We introduce NL2Scratch, an executable benchmark for natural-language-to-Scratch generation comprising 311,648 parser-valid NL--program pairs, whose program side is extracted from real Scratch projects and paired with semantically aligned NL descriptions. For reliable evaluation beyond surface overlap, we propose Semantic Alignment Consistency (SAC), an interpretable slot-level metric for measuring semantic agreement between descriptions and programs. With SAC, we construct a semantically validated pool of 23,594 examples, and a slot-balanced 800 diagnostic benchmark. Experiments across instruction-tuned and fine-tuned LLMs reveal a notable gap between lexical similarity and semantic alignment: models achieving token-level F1 above 0.93 often fail to attain perfect SAC, particularly on longer examples. Errors concentrate on operational slots like actions, conditions, and numeric arguments, exposing failure modes largely invisible under conventional metrics.
2606.22061.paper_summary_zh: 區塊式編程環境如 Scratch 在早期編程教育中被廣泛使用，但自然語言轉代碼 (NL2Code) 的研究主要集中在基於文本的語言上。Scratch 程式是事件驅動的、視覺組合的，並且分佈在並行的腳本中，這使得傳統的 NL2Code 假設和評估不足。我們介紹 NL2Scratch，這是一個可執行的基準，用於自然語言到 Scratch 的生成，包含 311,648 對解析器有效的 NL--程式對，其程式部分來自真實的 Scratch 項目，並與語義對齊的 NL 描述配對。為了在表面重疊之外進行可靠的評估，我們提出了語義對齊一致性 (SAC)，這是一種可解釋的槽級指標，用於測量描述與程式之間的語義一致性。通過 SAC，我們構建了一個語義驗證的 23,594 個示例的池，以及一個槽平衡的 800 個診斷基準。在針對指令調整和微調的 LLMs 進行的實驗中，顯示出詞彙相似性和語義對齊之間的顯著差距：達到 0.93 以上的令牌級 F1 的模型通常無法獲得完美的 SAC，特別是在較長的示例中。錯誤集中在操作槽上，如動作、條件和數值參數，揭示了在傳統指標下幾乎不可見的失敗模式。
2606.22061.author: Heejin Do et.al.
2606.22061.authors: Heejin Do, Alexandre Ballenghien, Yang Wu, April Yi Wang
